steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The decisive clue is Halliwell’s claim that “imitation” is ‘overly reductive’ because it suggests art is ‘entirely derivative’ of an external reality—hinting that the correct answer will show mimesis involves something beyond mere copying.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: D directly shows how mimesis was understood as art’s ability to envision hypothetical conditions (e.g., ‘could, but don’t yet, exist’), which contradicts the reductive idea that art merely copies reality. A, B, and C either describe etymology, psychological effects, or chronology, without addressing the conceptual nuance Halliwell critiques. That matches choice D — D is correct.
